title: rosa tomentella subvar. canescens
intro: Rosa tomentella subvar. canescens, also known as the hairy rose, is a subspecies of flowering shrub in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Rosa tomentella subvar. canescens include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving pain, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Rosa tomentella subvar. canescens is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
